title: 7 Best Herbal Teas For Hair Fall
intro: Herbal teas for Hair fall are natural remedies made from various plant extracts, designed to promote healthy hair growth and prevent excessive shedding. These teas can effectively treat hair fall due to their rich antioxidant properties, which help protect the scalp from damage, reduce inflammation, and improve blood circulation. Some popular herbal teas for hair fall include peppermint tea, which cools the scalp and stimulates hair growth, rosemary tea, which improves circulation and reduces dandruff, and sage tea, which balances scalp pH and reduces hair fall. Additionally, other herbal teas like lavender tea, chamomile tea, and green tea can also help, as they soothe the scalp, promote relaxation, and provide essential nutrients for hair growth.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, also known as rosemary tea, have been traditionally used to treat hair fall due to their stimulating and antioxidant properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat hair fall by improving circulation to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth, and reducing oxidative stress caused by free radicals. Bioactive constituents such as carnosic acid, rosmarinic acid, and caffeic acid in rosemary tea are responsible for its therapeutic effects, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities that help to soothe and protect the scalp. The benefits of rosemary tea in treating hair fall include reducing dandruff, promoting a healthy scalp environment, and enhancing the overall appearance of hair, making it a natural and effective remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a separate cup and discard the leaves.
Add 2 tablespoons of honey or sugar to sweeten the tea, if desired.
Let the tea cool down before using it as a hair mask to prevent hair fall.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be effective in treating hair fall, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications, especially blood thinners. To use Rosmarinus officinalis teas safely, take it in moderation and avoid consuming it with other stimulants like caffeine, as this may exacerbate stomach issues and lead to insomnia or anxiety.
preparation_usage: Wash your hair with a mild shampoo to start.
Mix 2 teaspoons of Rosmarinus officinalis tea with 1 cup of water.
Apply the mixture to your scalp and massage for 5 minutes.
Leave the mixture on for 30 minutes to an hour before rinsing.
Repeat the process 2-3 times a week for best results.

preparation_amazon.title: Palm Beach Medicinal Herbs - Rosemary Tea - Pure Herbal Tea Series, 30ct

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas have been used for centuries to treat hair fall ailments due to its rich properties that promote hair growth and reduce shedding. The herbal preparation helps to treat hair fall by reducing inflammation and promoting blood circulation to the scalp, thereby strengthening hair follicles and promoting new hair growth. Aloe barbadensis teas contain bioactive constituents such as vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium, that help to nourish and moisturize the scalp, reducing hair fall and promoting healthy hair growth. The benefits of Aloe barbadensis teas in treating hair fall include reduced shedding, improved hair texture, and increased hair density, making it a natural and effective remedy for hair loss.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of water, 1/4 cup of Aloe barbadensis gel, and 1 tablespoon of honey.
Boil the water in a pot and let it cool for 10 minutes.
Add the Aloe gel and honey to the cooled water. Stir well.
Heat the mixture for 2-3 minutes, then remove from heat and let it cool.
Strain the mixture and store it in the fridge. Use 1/4 cup of the tea as a hair mask, applied for 30 minutes before washing your hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe barbadensis teas can help treat hair fall ailments, but its possible side effects may include stomach upset, diarrhea, or allergic reactions due to its potent properties. To minimize these risks, it's recommended to consume the tea in moderation and avoid using it if you have sensitive skin or allergies, and also be cautious when combining it with other medications.
preparation_usage: Wash your hair with a mild shampoo before use.
Apply 2 tablespoons of Aloe barbadensis tea to your scalp.
Massage gently for 5 minutes to stimulate blood flow.
Leave it on for 30 minutes to an hour before rinsing.
Use once a week for 2-3 months for best results.

preparation_amazon.title: The Republic of Tea Beautifying Botanicals® Clean Beauty Berry Aloe Herbal Tea Bags (36 count)

preparations.herb_desc: Urtica dioica teas, also known as nettle root tea, have been used to treat hair fall due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat hair fall by reducing inflammation in the scalp, promoting hair growth, and strengthening hair follicles. The bioactive constituents of Urtica dioica, including flavonoids, saponins, and triterpenoids, contribute to its hair growth-promoting effects by reducing oxidative stress and improving scalp health. Regular consumption of Urtica dioica teas has been shown to promote hair growth, reduce hair loss, and improve overall scalp health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Urtica dioica leaves. If using fresh, wash them with water and pat dry.
Combine 1 tablespoon of Urtica dioica leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey or sugar to the tea if desired, then stir well.
Drink the tea 1-2 times a day for 2-3 months to see results. You can also use it as a hair rinse by applying it to your hair after shampooing and rinsing with cold water.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Current pharmaceutical design", Urtica dioica teas for hair fall have been shown to be effective in reducing hair loss due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Urtica dioica teas can be used to treat hair fall, but its use may be associated with side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions to take when using Urtica dioica teas for hair fall treatment include avoiding its use in people with sensitive stomachs, pregnant women, and those with kidney problems, as it may exacerbate these conditions.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Urtica dioica tea 2 times a day
Use 2 tablespoons of dried Urtica dioica leaves per cup
Steep in boiling water for 5-7 minutes
Take for 2-3 months for noticeable hair growth results
Combine with a balanced diet for optimal hair care

preparation_amazon.title: The Republic of Tea — Organic Nettle SuperHerb Tea Tin, 36 Herbal Tea Bags, Naturally Caffeine-Free

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas, also known as turmeric tea, have been traditionally used to treat hair fall due to their potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The curcumin present in this herbal preparation helps to treat hair fall by reducing inflammation and promoting hair growth, while also improving blood circulation to the scalp. The bioactive constituents of Curcuma longa teas, such as curcuminoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, help to inhibit the 5-alpha-reductase enzyme, which is responsible for converting testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to hair loss. The benefits of using Curcuma longa teas to treat hair fall include reduced hair shedding, improved hair texture, and enhanced hair growth, making it a natural and effective remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Boil 2 cups of water in a pot.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried Curcuma longa roots to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and let it cool.
Drink the tea once a day to help reduce hair fall.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be beneficial in treating hair fall, but it may also cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Curcuma longa teas safely, it is essential to start with a low dose and gradually increase it, as well as to avoid consuming it in large quantities or for extended periods, which may lead to liver damage or interact with other medications.
preparation_usage: Mix 1 teaspoon of Curcuma longa powder with 1 cup of warm water.
Drink the mixture twice a day, morning and evening.
Use 2 tablespoons of Curcuma longa powder as a hair mask.
Leave the hair mask on for 30 minutes to an hour before rinsing.
Use this treatment once a week for 3-4 months for noticeable results.

preparations.herb_desc: Equisetum arvense teas, also known as horsetail tea, have been traditionally used to treat hair fall ailments due to their rich properties that promote hair growth and strengthen hair follicles. The tea is rich in silica, which is an essential mineral that helps to strengthen hair follicles, improve hair texture, and reduce breakage, thereby treating hair fall effectively. The bioactive constituents of Equisetum arvense teas, including flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, have been found to promote hair growth by improving blood circulation, reducing inflammation, and enhancing the overall health of the scalp. The benefits of using Equisetum arvense teas to treat hair fall include a reduction in hair loss, improved hair texture, and a noticeable increase in hair growth, making it a popular natural remedy for hair care.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Equisetum arvense roots and stems. Clean them with water.
Dry the Equisetum arvense roots and stems in a low-temperature oven (150°F - 200°F) for 1 hour.
Steep 1 tablespoon of dried Equisetum arvense in 2 cups of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool. Store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
Drink 1 cup of the Equisetum arvense tea once a day to help prevent hair fall. Use it for 2-3 months to see noticeable results.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Equisetum arvense teas can be an effective treatment for hair fall, but it may cause allergic reactions, skin irritation, or interact with certain medications, especially blood thinners and diabetes medications. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with small amounts and gradually increase the dosage, avoid using Equisetum arvense teas if you have a history of kidney or liver issues, and monitor your body's response closely to adjust the usage accordingly.
preparation_usage: Use 1-2 tablespoons of Equisetum arvense tea leaves per cup.
Boil water and steep the tea for 5-10 minutes.
Drink 1 cup of the tea twice a day, morning and evening.
Continue using the tea for 2-3 months for noticeable results.
Combine with a balanced diet and regular hair care for best results.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to help treat hair fall ailments due to their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and vasodilatory properties. The flavonoids and bilobalide present in these teas help to improve blood circulation to the scalp, promote hair growth, and reduce dandruff, thereby addressing the underlying causes of hair fall. The bioactive constituents, including quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, in Ginkgo biloba teas also exhibit anti-androgenic effects, which can help to prevent hair loss caused by hormonal imbalances. Regular consumption of Ginkgo biloba teas has been reported to promote hair growth, reduce hair fall, and improve overall scalp health, making it a popular natural remedy for hair loss prevention.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, 1 cup of water, and a teapot.
Boil the water in the teapot and pour it over the dried Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Let the leaves steep in the water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the leaves. Add 1 tablespoon of honey to sweeten, if desired.
Drink 1 cup of the Ginkgo biloba tea twice a day to promote hair growth and reduce hair fall.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be used to treat hair fall ailments, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset due to its blood-thinning properties. To avoid these side effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase it, and to be cautious when taking Ginkgo biloba teas with other medications, especially blood thinners and antidepressants.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea 2 times a day.
Use 1 tablespoon of Ginkgo biloba powder per cup.
Steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbs to infuse.
Consume for 3-6 months to see noticeable hair growth.
Combine with a balanced diet and regular exercise for best results.

preparations.herb_desc: Avena sativa teas, derived from the oat plant, have been used as a natural remedy to treat hair fall ailments. The properties of this herbal preparation help to treat hair fall by promoting hair growth, reducing stress, and soothing scalp irritation. The bioactive constituents, including avenanthramides and avenacosides, help to treat hair fall by reducing inflammation, promoting blood circulation, and strengthening hair follicles. The benefits of Avena sativa teas in treating hair fall include promoting healthy hair growth, reducing hair loss, and improving overall scalp health, making it a popular natural remedy for hair care.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Avena sativa, 2 cups of water, and a strainer.
Rinse the Avena sativa and chop it into small pieces.
Boil the 2 cups of water in a pot and add the chopped Avena sativa.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes. Strain the mixture using a strainer.
Let the tea cool down, then store it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. Use 1 tablespoon of the tea as a hair mask or rinse your hair with it after shampooing.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Avena sativa teas for hair fall may work by targeting both primary (androgens and inheritance) and secondary (microinflammation) aetiological factors in androgenetic alopecia, possibly due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, or dual inhibition of key enzymes properties.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Avena sativa teas can be beneficial in treating hair fall, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Avena sativa teas safely, it is essential to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming it during pregnancy or breastfeeding, as well as when taking medications that may interact with its active ingredients.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Avena sativa tea 2-3 times a week.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Avena sativa per cup of water.
Gradually increase dosage to 2 teaspoons per cup if needed.
Use Avena sativa tea for 2-3 months to see noticeable results.
Combine Avena sativa tea with a balanced diet and regular hair care.

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas, also known as lemon balm teas, have been traditionally used to treat hair fall ailments due to their soothing and calming properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation, and balancing the hormonal system, which in turn helps to prevent hair loss. The bioactive constituents of Melissa officinalis teas, including rosmarinic acid, eugenol, and citral, have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the hair follicles from damage and promote healthy hair growth. The benefits of using Melissa officinalis teas to treat hair fall ailments include promoting a healthy scalp, reducing inflammation, and enhancing the overall well-being, leading to stronger and thicker hair.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers.
Combine the leaves and flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a saucepan.
Reduce heat and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea to sweeten it, if desired.
Let the tea cool, then use it as a hair rinse to help prevent hair fall.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be used as a potential natural remedy to treat hair fall, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, headaches, or allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive skin or allergies. To use Melissa officinalis teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose, monitor your body's response, and avoid consuming it if you experience any adverse reactions, and also to be aware of potential interactions with other medications or health conditions, such as low blood pressure.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas, also known as milk thistle tea, have been traditionally used to treat hair fall due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tea helps to treat hair fall by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth. The bioactive constituents of Silybum marianum, including silymarin and flavonoids, help to protect the hair follicles from damage and promote the growth of new hair. The benefits of Silybum marianum tea in treating hair fall include reduced shedding, strengthened hair follicles, and improved overall hair health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Silybum marianum flowers and 2 cups of water.
Combine flowers and water in a saucepan, and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the flowers. Let it cool.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a week, for best results for hair fall.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be used to treat hair fall ailment, but it may cause gastrointestinal side effects such as nausea, diarrhea, and stomach cramps due to its high saponin content. To use Silybum marianum teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase it, and to avoid consuming it on an empty stomach to minimize the risk of digestive issues.

preparations.herb_desc: Cassia auriculata teas have been traditionally used to treat hair fall ailments due to their rich antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to promote hair growth and reduce hair loss. The bioactive constituents present in Cassia auriculata teas, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, help to nourish the scalp, strengthen hair follicles, and prevent hair fall. The herbal preparation helps to treat hair fall by regulating the hair growth cycle, improving blood circulation to the scalp, and reducing stress-induced hair loss. The benefits of Cassia auriculata teas in treating hair fall include promoting healthy hair growth, reducing dandruff and itchiness, and improving the overall texture and appearance of the hair.
preparation_procedure: Gather Cassia auriculata flowers (1 cup) and boil 2 cups of water in a pot.
Add the Cassia auriculata flowers to the boiling water and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the flowers. Let the liquid cool down to room temperature.
Once cooled, use the liquid as a hair wash. Massage your scalp with it and let it stay for 30 minutes.
Rinse your hair with warm water and repeat the process 2-3 times a week for best results.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cassia auriculata teas can be used to treat hair fall, but consuming it may lead to side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To minimize risks, it is essential to use Cassia auriculata teas in moderation, avoid mixing it with other herbal preparations, and monitor your body's response closely, especially if you are pregnant or breastfeeding.

preparations.herb_desc: Panax ginseng teas have been traditionally used to treat hair fall ailments due to their adaptogenic and antioxidant properties, which help to promote hair growth and reduce shedding. The bioactive constituents of Panax ginseng teas, including ginsenosides and saponins, play a crucial role in treating hair fall by stimulating hair follicle growth, improving blood circulation, and reducing inflammation. The benefits of using Panax ginseng teas for hair fall treatment include increased hair thickness, reduced shedding, and improved overall scalp health. Regular consumption of Panax ginseng teas can also help to alleviate stress and promote a healthy hair growth cycle.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried Panax ginseng root.
Boil the water in a pot and let it cool for 1-2 minutes.
Add the dried Panax ginseng root to the cooled water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the Panax ginseng root.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a week to help promote hair growth and reduce hair fall.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Panax ginseng teas can cause potential side effects such as insomnia, restlessness, and anxiety in some individuals when used to treat hair fall, and may also interact with certain medications, including blood thinners and diabetes medications. To minimize risks, individuals using Panax ginseng teas for hair fall should be cautious of their caffeine intake and avoid consuming the teas in large quantities, especially in the afternoon or evening to prevent disrupting their sleep patterns.

preparations.herb_desc: Camellia sinensis teas, commonly known as green tea, have been traditionally used to treat hair fall ailments due to their properties that promote healthy hair growth and reduce hair loss. The antioxidant properties of green tea help to protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthy scalp and reducing hair fall. The bioactive constituents of green tea, such as catechins and flavonoids, help to improve blood circulation to the scalp, reduce inflammation, and prevent hair loss. Regular consumption of Camellia sinensis teas has been found to promote hair growth, reduce hair fall, and improve overall scalp health, making it a beneficial herbal preparation for treating hair fall ailments.
preparation_procedure: Boil 2 cups of water in a pot.
Add 2 tablespoons of Camellia sinensis (black tea leaves) to a separate cup.
Pour the boiled water over the tea leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the tea leaves.
Let the tea cool down, then use it as a final rinse after shampooing your hair to help reduce hair fall.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of biological regulators and homeostatic agents, Camellia sinensis teas for hair fall may be beneficial due to its high phenolic and flavonoid content, particularly the 50% methanolic extract which was found to have a significant antioxidant capacity with a radical scavenging DPPH assay of 78.95±7.12%.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Camellia sinensis teas can be beneficial in treating hair fall due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, but it may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, or interactions with certain medications. preparations.preparation_study: According to the study in Journal of ethnopharmacology, Zingiber officinale teas for hair fall were not specifically mentioned, but Zingiber officinale was found to be one of the most used plants (with the highest level of relative frequency of citation of 1.0) in treating various diseases, primarily gastrointestinal diseases.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can help alleviate hair fall issues when consumed, but possible side effects may include digestive discomfort, nausea, and dizziness due to its high ginger content. To use Zingiber officinale teas effectively for hair growth, it is recommended to consume it in moderation, ideally after meals, to minimize stomach upset and interact with other medications, and to avoid excessive consumption, especially during pregnancy or breastfeeding.

preparations.herb_desc: Cassia angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat hair fall ailments due to their rich properties that help to promote hair growth and strengthen hair follicles. The herbal preparation of Cassia angustifolia contains bioactive constituents such as saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which help to treat hair fall by promoting blood circulation, reducing oxidative stress, and inhibiting the activity of 5-alpha-reductase enzyme that contributes to hair loss. The saponins present in Cassia angustifolia teas help to nourish the hair roots, improve the texture and strength of hair, and reduce the shedding of hair. By consuming Cassia angustifolia teas, individuals can benefit from its ability to promote hair growth, reduce hair fall, and enhance the overall health and appearance of their hair.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Cassia angustifolia leaves and 2 cups of water.
Clean the Cassia angustifolia leaves and boil 2 cups of water in a pot.
Add 1 cup of Cassia angustifolia leaves to the boiling water and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool down. Keep it in the refrigerator for later use.
To use the tea, pour 1/4 cup of the cooled tea into a cup and mix it with 1 cup of water. Use it as a final rinse after shampooing your hair.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the given study, I can't answer for hair fall using Cassia angustifolia teas as hair fall is not mentioned in the study, but it mentions laxative potential of the plant.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cassia angustifolia teas can be an effective herbal remedy for treating hair fall, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and gastrointestinal issues like nausea and diarrhea in some individuals. To use Cassia angustifolia teas safely, it is recommended to consume it in moderation, avoid using it with other medications, and be cautious when applying it topically to the scalp to minimize the risk of skin irritation and allergic reactions.

preparations.herb_desc: Mentha x piperita teas, also known as peppermint tea, have been traditionally used to treat hair fall due to their unique properties. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of peppermint tea help to soothe the scalp and promote a healthy hair growth environment, thereby reducing hair fall. The bioactive constituents of peppermint tea, including menthol, menthone, and limonene, exhibit their hair growth-promoting effects by increasing blood flow to the scalp, reducing dandruff and itchiness, and inhibiting the activity of 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme responsible for hair loss. The benefits of using peppermint tea to treat hair fall include a reduced risk of hair loss, improved scalp health, and a natural, non-invasive treatment option that can be easily incorporated into one's daily routine.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Mentha x piperita leaves, 1 cup of water, and a strainer.
Boil the water in a pot and then pour it over the leaves in a cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the leaves to release their flavor and benefits.
Strain the liquid into another cup to remove the leaves and discard them.
Cool the tea and use it as a final rinse after shampooing your hair to help reduce hair fall.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Mentha x piperita teas can be beneficial in treating hair fall ailment, but they may cause stomach upset and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive skin or digestive issues. To use Mentha x piperita teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming it before surgery or if you have a history of bleeding disorders, as it may thin the blood and affect clotting.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinnamomum verum teas have been traditionally used to treat hair fall ailment due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antifungal activities. The bioactive constituents present in Cinnamomum verum teas, such as cinnamaldehyde, eugenol, and linalool, help to promote hair growth and reduce hair fall by strengthening hair follicles and improving scalp health. Regular consumption of Cinnamomum verum teas has been found to help in treating hair fall by reducing stress, promoting relaxation, and improving overall well-being, thereby reducing the risk of hair loss. The benefits of using Cinnamomum verum teas to treat hair fall include a reduction in hair fall rate, improved hair texture, and a healthy scalp, making it a natural and effective remedy for hair care.
preparation_procedure: Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle or on the stovetop.
Add 2 tablespoons of Cinnamomum verum bark to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the bark.
Use the tea as a final rinse after shampooing your hair to help prevent hair fall.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Daru : journal of Faculty of Pharmacy, Tehran University of Medical Sciences, Cinnamomum verum teas are not mentioned for hair fall but for use in treating PCOS, however there is no mention of Cinnamomum verum teas for hair fall.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinnamomum verum teas can be beneficial in treating hair fall, but excessive consumption may lead to side effects such as stomach discomfort, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Cinnamomum verum teas safely, it is essential to start with small amounts and gradually increase the dose, as well as avoid consuming it on an empty stomach and not to use it for extended periods.

preparation_study: According to International journal of molecular sciences, Rosmarinus officinalis teas for hair fall may stimulate new hair growth due to the presence of flavonoids that can promote dermal microcirculation and help in repairing or recovering from hair follicle disruption.
intro_study: According to "Current pharmaceutical design", teas for hair fall may be effective in reducing hair loss due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can improve local metabolism when applied externally.
